samples.xml_description:
Design a pulse detection unit. The input signal data_in is triggered by clk. When data_in changes from 0 to 1 to 0 (3 cycles), it is considered a pulse. The indicating signal data_out is 1 at the end of the pulse, and returns to 0 until the next pulse. Clock signal. Reset signal (active low). 1-bit input signal. Output signal indicating the presence of pulses. Idle state. State 1. State 2. State 3. State register.

Declare a register state to keep track of the current state of the pulse detection process.

Inside an always block, sensitive to the positive edge of the clk signal and the negative edge of the rst_n signal, implement the logic for pulse detection and output generation.

In the reset condition (~rst_n), set the state register to the initial state and set the data_out output to 0, indicating no pulse.

Continue the detection process for the remaining states. For each state, check the value of data_in and transition to the next state accordingly.

If the current state satisfies the last state of a "pulse", set data_out to 1, indicating the end of a pulse. Otherwise, set data_out to 0.
